transcript.text: That I didn't want to be part of the society, and I was eating meat three times a day. I loved it.
I just said, this seems like the way to go, and I had read a book called
Mucous-less Diet Healing System by Arnold Aaron, and he talked about what happened to your body
As he changed from a meat protein thing. He described all kinds of bodily functions.
I can do three or four hours on that if you want, but I experienced it, and I really went through it,
And my body got lighter, and I felt better, and after about two or three years of doing it,
Actually, it was a year when all of a sudden it became, I have respect for nasty cows.
I have respect for these animals that we were eating, where I really was taking it for granted
And just eating them. Then I ended up getting that respect for all lives, you know,
And at that time, it was also, I started moving into a different kind of spiritual thought,
Different kinds of different kinds of stuff, you know, I just kind of dabbled in, you know,
I really believed that, you know, God is willing to tell everyone who wants to get there is appropriate to me,
You know, like, it's not even my fault.
There are many paths to get there.
Yeah, definitely.
You know, over the years, I've owned two farms. One was a 20-acre farm in Bowlerville, Michigan,
And the other one was near Springfield, Illinois. That was 114 acres. Gosh, I wish I stood there.
Were you growing anything?
Well, actually, the farm was, I rented it out. We had about 70 acres that we rented out to a local farmer
Who planted corn and soybeans. And, you know, then we'd rotate the fields over here.
And I owned a place for about four years. It must be worth a fortune today.
But it was kind of neat, John, every year to watch what he did from the growing to the harvesting.
You know, he had these huge combines. He had everything.
Right.
How many years ago did this George?
Oh, it was 19...
It must have been 15, 20 years ago.
Oh, yeah, yeah.
But it was just great to watch. I mean, it was just amazing to watch him do that.
And then they take the seeds to this...
